search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae Eustachian tube dysfunction guideline UAE
site:entnet.org Eustachian tube dysfunction clinical consensus adult
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng98 hearing loss otoscopy tympanometry vertigo discharge
rejection_reasons: Procedure-specific statements do not support a broad documentation workflow.
Paediatric-only guidance was not generalized to adults.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Limited to adults over 18 with hearing difficulty or hearing loss; Eustachian-tube dysfunction identity, children, and flight-related pressure symptoms are not exact source matches.
setting_applicability: Partial for primary-care hearing assessment, otoscopy, audiology, tympanometry, and referral prompts; diagnosis and treatment remain unsupported.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE Eustachian-tube-dysfunction guideline was identified. NICE referral and service pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98, last reviewed 2023-10-02 with a minor link update in 2025, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer superseding NICE adult hearing-loss guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports muffled or reduced hearing plus ear pain, discharge, and neurological warning documentation without assigning an Eustachian-tube cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports persistent or asymmetric hearing, vertigo, otalgia, discharge, abnormal ear findings, and clinician-led referral prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports otoscopy, hearing assessment, tympanometry when indicated, audiology review, and personalised discussion without treatment advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: Eustachian-tube-dysfunction identity, ear pressure, popping, blocked-ear sensation, flight trigger, fever, middle-ear effusion, tuning-fork tests, pressure-equalisation advice, watchful waiting, fixed review timing, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer Eustachian-tube dysfunction or recommend steroids, antibiotics, pressure manoeuvres, or referral.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
